<!-- Generated from sibling .json by scripts/spec-trace/Render-SpecTraceMarkdownFromJson.ps1. Do not edit this file directly. -->  # ARC-QUIC-CRT-0004 - QUIC Connection Runtime Lifecycle and Idle Timeout Architecture  ## Purpose  Describe the runtime-owned lifecycle seam that derives idle timeout, enters Closing and Draining, and emits explicit close, drain, timer, and discard effects without inline I/O.  ## Requirements Satisfied  - REQ-QUIC-CRT-0023 - REQ-QUIC-CRT-0024 - REQ-QUIC-CRT-0025 - REQ-QUIC-CRT-0026 - REQ-QUIC-CRT-0027 - REQ-QUIC-CRT-0030 - REQ-QUIC-CRT-0031 - REQ-QUIC-CRT-0032 - REQ-QUIC-CRT-0035 - REQ-QUIC-CRT-0055 - REQ-QUIC-CRT-0056 - REQ-QUIC-CRT-0074 - REQ-QUIC-CRT-0075 - REQ-QUIC-CRT-0076 - REQ-QUIC-CRT-0077 - REQ-QUIC-CRT-0078 - REQ-QUIC-CRT-0084 - REQ-QUIC-CRT-0087  ## Design Summary  The runtime remains the single owner of lifecycle ordering. It derives the effective idle timeout through the helper state, restarts the idle deadline only on allowed peer activity, transitions to Closing on local immediate close, transitions to Draining on received CONNECTION_CLOSE or accepted stateless reset, and arms or cancels close, drain, and idle timers through explicit effects.  Ordinary send legality is surfaced as a phase-derived property so callers do not need to infer it from scattered helpers. When the connection reaches a terminal lifetime boundary, the runtime discards connection-owned state, notifies live streams of the terminal state, and emits the discard effect as an explicit outcome.  The close/drain lifetime must preserve its end time when Draining follows Closing, and monotonic time remains the only source for timeout and deadline calculations.  ## Key Components  - src/Incursa.Quic/QuicConnectionRuntime.cs - src/Incursa.Quic/QuicConnectionRuntimeEventModels.cs - src/Incursa.Quic/QuicConnectionRuntimeStateModels.cs - src/Incursa.Quic/QuicIdleTimeoutState.cs - src/Incursa.Quic/QuicConnectionStreamRegistry.cs  ## Risks  - Timer conversion must stay monotonic and saturating so close and idle deadlines never move backward. - Draining after Closing must preserve the existing terminal end tick or a peer close could shorten the drain period. - Close-payload formatting must remain deterministic so the send effect stays reproducible across hosts.  ## Lifecycle Model  The runtime keeps a single phase value as the source of truth for ordinary sending, close-only sending, or no sending. Idle timeout state is created only when both transport-parameter sides and the PTO floor produce an effective timeout. Closing retains close-relevant state and arms a close lifetime. Draining preserves the existing terminal end time when entered from Closing. Discard clears lifecycle timers and emits explicit discard effects.  ## Effect Contract  Local close emits a close send effect when a path is available. Received CONNECTION_CLOSE and matched stateless reset suppress close retransmission and enter Draining. Timer expirations drive close-lifetime, drain-lifetime, and idle-timeout transitions back through the same runtime reducer. Terminal transitions notify live streams before the runtime drops connection-owned state.
